front.title: Object-Oriented Programming Standard for Pure C

abstract.t:
This document defines RFC-OOP-C, a standard for object-oriented programming in pure C. It specifies naming conventions, class structure macros, method macros, validation macros, memory allocation patterns, lifecycle management, build-level configuration, struct versioning, Doxygen documentation requirements, and a conformance checker interface (the RFC-OOP-C Conformance Checker, RFC_Checker).
The standard targets C11 (ISO/IEC 9899:2011) and is applicable to any C project on any microcontroller or platform whose toolchain supports C11. Supplementary guidance is provided for embedded systems including thread-safety and memory allocation strategies compatible with bare-metal, FreeRTOS and embOS.
